tiers.tier_1: MVP — every PRD §6 feature + WCAG 2.2 AA + tests + Chrome DevTools verification
tiers.tier_2: Deferred per PRD §10 (recent-downloads list, FLAC/ALAC formats, bulk-paste queue, mobile FAB, etc.)

critical_path_note: Backend foundation (DL-001 → DL-045) must precede frontend integration (DL-050 → DL-105). A11y (DL-110 → DL-118) overlaps frontend. Tests (DL-120 → DL-140) run alongside. Verification (DL-153) is the final gate.

buckets.F: Frontend — Reveal + Success actions (DL-100 to DL-105)

tasks.title: Create CHANGELOG.md and bump pyproject 0.1.0 → 0.2.0
tasks.description: Create /CHANGELOG.md with v0.2.0 entry listing the Download refactor. Add pre-emptive '[BREAKING — planned for 0.3.0]' entry covering: removal of POST /api/download legacy alias, removal of POST /api/download/album legacy alias, removal of legacy format coercion table. Bump pyproject.toml version field.

tasks.title: Create tests/fixtures/download_errors/ skeleton
tasks.description: Create directory with empty placeholder .txt files for each error code listed in PRD §6.7: unavailable_video, age_gated, region_blocked, private_video, network_offline, network_timeout, disk_full, ffmpeg_conversion, ffmpeg_missing, unknown. README.md in the directory explaining the fixture format + how to capture new ones.

tasks.title: Create docs/reference/youtube-download.md scaffold
tasks.description: Create the reference doc with sections: '# YouTube Download', '## Format migration' (legacy → new mapping table, audio_quality removal), '## yt-dlp upgrade' (4-step checklist + canonical fixture URLs), '## Browser support' (Safari 15.4 / Firefox 121 / Chromium 105 baseline, Firefox <121 fallback caveat).

tasks.title: Add Download must-know constraint to CLAUDE.md
tasks.description: Append one bullet under 'Must-know constraints (read every session)' covering: AUTOCUE_DOWNLOAD_CONCURRENCY env (default 1, max 4); job queue + cancel endpoint architecture; ffmpeg subprocess.Popen.terminate() path for mid-loudnorm cancel; output_artifacts cleanup with mtime gate.

tasks.title: Add jsonschema dev dep for DownloadProgressEvent test
tasks.description: Ensure pydantic v2 model_validate is wired in tests; no new runtime dep needed (pydantic already installed). Just confirm pyproject.toml [project.optional-dependencies] dev section includes httpx for SSE consumption in pytest.

tasks.title: Add _ytdlp_format_opts(audio_format) mapping
tasks.description: Pure function returning dict that callers merge into yt-dlp opts. 'wav' → FFmpegExtractAudio postprocessor preferredcodec=wav. 'mp3_320' → preferredcodec=mp3, preferredquality=320. 'original' → no postprocessor (yt-dlp keeps bestaudio/best container).

tasks.title: Refactor download_audio() signature
tasks.description: Drop audio_quality positional/keyword arg. Add audio_format='mp3_320' default, allow_playlist=False, embed_metadata=True, normalize_lufs: float|None=None. Preserve progress_cb + cancel_event params. Internal: use _ytdlp_format_opts() for codec; if normalize_lufs is not None call normalize_loudness_to_lufs() post-download; if allow_playlist=True remove noplaylist key from opts.

tasks.title: Drop hardcoded noplaylist=True; gate on allow_playlist
tasks.description: Inside download_audio(), build the opts dict conditionally: opts['noplaylist'] = not allow_playlist. Tests fail if noplaylist is still always True.

tasks.title: Add expand_playlist(url) helper
tasks.description: yt-dlp extract_info(download=False, extract_flat=True). Returns list[{query: webpage_url, title}] for each entry. Empty list if not a playlist URL or extract fails. Catches yt-dlp.utils.DownloadError + general Exception.
pass_criteria: test_expand_playlist returns ≥ 2 entries for a known playlist (vcr or mocked)

tasks.title: Add embed_metadata postprocessors (FFmpegMetadata + MetadataParser)
tasks.description: When embed_metadata=True, append to opts['postprocessors']: {'key': 'FFmpegMetadata', 'add_metadata': True} and {'key': 'MetadataParser', 'actions': [(MetadataParserPP.interpretter, 'title', regex_for_artist_title)]}. Regex: (?P<artist>.+?) ?[-–—] ?(?P<title>.+). Falls through cleanly when separator absent.
pass_criteria: with embed_metadata=True, opts['postprocessors'] contains FFmpegMetadata + MetadataParser keys

tasks.title: Add normalize_loudness_to_lufs() two-pass
tasks.description: Pass 1: ffmpeg -i src -af loudnorm=I=-14:LRA=11:TP=-1:print_format=json -f null -. Parse measured_I/TP/LRA/thresh/offset from stderr. Pass 2: ffmpeg -i src -af loudnorm=...:measured_*=...:linear=true:print_format=summary -c:a <codec> dst -progress pipe:1. Codec by extension: .wav → pcm_s16le, .mp3 → libmp3lame -b:a 320k. Returns dst path; raises RuntimeError on ffmpeg non-zero exit. Takes optional progress_cb(percent).
pass_criteria: test_normalize_loudness_to_lufs on a 5-second sine wav returns a new file ≤ -14 LUFS ± 1 (measured by re-running ffmpeg loudnorm pass-1)

tasks.title: Parse ffmpeg -progress pipe:1 output for pass-2 percent
tasks.description: Helper _parse_ffmpeg_progress(line) returns float|None percent from 'out_time_ms=...' lines, given known total_duration_ms. Used inside normalize_loudness_to_lufs() pass-2.

tasks.title: Add subprocess.Popen handle for cancel-mid-ffmpeg
tasks.description: Wrap ffmpeg invocations in a context that registers the Popen handle and spawns a watcher thread checking cancel_event every 100ms; on set, proc.terminate() + 2s grace + proc.kill(). Used by normalize_loudness_to_lufs() pass-1 and pass-2.
pass_criteria: test_normalize_cancel: set cancel_event mid-pass-1, assert proc exits within 3s, no orphan process

tasks.title: Add output_artifacts tracking + mtime-gated cleanup
tasks.description: Each job creates output_artifacts: set[Path]. Populated via yt-dlp progress hook (status='finished' → add file path) and ffmpeg subprocess wrapper (add output path). In job worker's finally:, iterate output_artifacts; if path != success_path AND path exists AND path.stat().st_mtime >= job.started_at → unlink. No glob sweeps.
pass_criteria: test_cleanup_on_cancel: cancel mid-download, assert .part file deleted
test_cleanup_preserves_preexisting: pre-create dest file with mtime < job.started_at, cancel job, assert pre-existing file untouched

tasks.title: Add classify_download_error(exc) taxonomy
tasks.description: Map exceptions to {code, user_message, hint, raw}. 10 codes per PRD §6.7. Pattern matching via regex on str(exc) + isinstance checks (OSError errno for disk_full/network_offline, socket.timeout for network_timeout, yt_dlp.utils.DownloadError + message regex for the YouTube-side codes).

tasks.title: Populate the 10 error fixtures with real yt-dlp output
tasks.description: Capture real stderr/exception strings from yt-dlp against known-bad URLs. Sources: yt-dlp test suite, manual capture against private video / age-gated / region-blocked / deleted URLs. Each fixture file contains ONE exemplar string. Document the source URL in a comment header.
pass_criteria: Each fixture is non-empty and has a # source: comment

tasks.title: Add DownloadQueue class skeleton
tasks.description: queue.Queue + worker thread + cancel_registry: dict[str, Event] + cancel_pending: set[str] + job event-bus (per-job queue.Queue for SSE events). Worker loop: dequeue → check cancel_pending → register active → run job → emit done → unregister. Concurrency from AUTOCUE_DOWNLOAD_CONCURRENCY env (default 1, clamp ≤ 4). ThreadPoolExecutor when > 1.
pass_criteria: DownloadQueue exposes enqueue(req) → job_id, cancel(job_id) → bool, event_stream(job_id) → generator
test_concurrent_enqueue: enqueue 5 jobs with concurrency=1, assert only 1 active at any time

tasks.title: Add queue_lock + cancel race discipline
tasks.description: Single threading.Lock wraps {dequeue → mark active → install cancel_event in registry}. cancel(job_id) acquires the lock: if job_id is active → registry[id].set(); else → cancel_pending.add(id). Worker's first action under the lock is: if job_id in cancel_pending → emit cancelled, skip. Defends round-3 B2 race.
pass_criteria: test_cancel_before_active: enqueue with concurrency=1, fill slot, enqueue 2nd, immediately cancel 2nd before worker picks up, assert 2nd emits status='cancelled' and is never processed

tasks.title: Add tiered watchdog (60s pulse, 30min stuck-phase)
tasks.description: Single watchdog thread per DownloadQueue ticks every 30s: if time.monotonic() - worker_pulse_at > 60: kill worker, emit done(error_code='worker_crash'). If time.monotonic() - last_event_at > 1800 AND phase in {fetching, normalizing}: kill, emit error_code='stuck_phase'. Otherwise: leave alone.
pass_criteria: test_watchdog_pulse: simulate worker silence > 60s, assert kill + done(worker_crash) event
test_watchdog_legitimate_long_job: legitimate phase events every 10min, watchdog never fires

tasks.title: Add worker-pulse sidecar thread
tasks.description: While inside subprocess wait (yt-dlp extract_info, ffmpeg pass-1/pass-2), spawn a sidecar daemon thread that touches worker_pulse_at = time.monotonic() every 5s as long as proc.poll() is None. Joins on subprocess completion.
pass_criteria: test_pulse_during_silence: run a long ffmpeg (slow filter on a long file), assert worker_pulse_at updates every ~5s with no SSE events emitted

tasks.title: Add AUTOCUE_DOWNLOAD_CONCURRENCY env reading
tasks.description: Read env once at DownloadQueue init: int(os.environ.get('AUTOCUE_DOWNLOAD_CONCURRENCY', '1')). Clamp to range [1, 4]. Log INFO line at startup with chosen value.
pass_criteria: AUTOCUE_DOWNLOAD_CONCURRENCY=8 autocue serve → log line shows max_concurrency=4
AUTOCUE_DOWNLOAD_CONCURRENCY=0 autocue serve → log line shows max_concurrency=1

tasks.title: Update DownloadRequest schema
tasks.description: audio_format: Literal['wav','mp3_320','original']='mp3_320'. normalize: bool=False. embed_metadata: bool=True. allow_playlist: bool=False. Remove audio_quality. model_config = ConfigDict(extra='forbid').

tasks.title: Update DownloadAlbumRequest schema
tasks.description: Same shape changes as DownloadRequest: audio_format default 'mp3_320', add normalize + embed_metadata, drop audio_quality, extra='forbid'.

tasks.title: Add DownloadProgressEvent model
tasks.description: Per PRD §7.2: type Literal[progress|done], phase Literal[queued|fetching|converting|normalizing|tagging]|None, percent float|None, processed int, total int, current_title str|None, current_query str|None, status Literal[success|error|cancelled]|None, path str|None, error_code str|None, error_message str|None, error_hint str|None, error_raw str|None, downloaded int, failed int, job_id str.

tasks.title: Add RevealPathRequest with path validation
tasks.description: Pydantic model with `path: str`. Custom validator: Path(path).resolve(strict=True); compute allowed_roots from default_download_dir() + _detect_music_folder(db) + AUTOCUE_DOWNLOAD_DIR env; assert any(resolved.is_relative_to(r) for r in allowed_roots); else raise ValueError with error_code='forbidden_path'.

tasks.title: Add 422 middleware for audio_quality_removed
tasks.description: FastAPI exception handler for RequestValidationError on download endpoints. If any error.type == 'extra_forbidden' AND error.loc[-1] == 'audio_quality': replace response body with {error_code:'audio_quality_removed', error_message:'audio_quality removed; use audio_format=mp3_320 for 320 kbps MP3 or wav for uncompressed.', hint:'Update your client to set audio_format instead.'}. Else pass through default.

tasks.title: Add legacy-format pre-validator coercion
tasks.description: Before pydantic validation, intercept the JSON body: if audio_format in legacy values, map per PRD §6.2 table (mp3 → mp3_320, m4a/aac/opus → original, flac/alac/vorbis/wav case-insensitive → wav). Re-inject into validated dict. Unknown values: let pydantic Literal validator 422 with friendly error_code='unknown_format'.
pass_criteria: POST /api/download/enqueue {query:'x', audio_format:'mp3'} returns 200 (coerced internally to mp3_320)

tasks.title: Log-once-per-(process, legacy_value) at WARNING
tasks.description: Module-level _legacy_format_seen: set[str]. On first occurrence of a legacy value, log WARNING with the mapping; subsequent occurrences log DEBUG. Resets only at process restart.
pass_criteria: test_log_once: caplog captures exactly 1 WARNING for 5 requests with audio_format='mp3'; 4 DEBUG entries

tasks.title: POST /api/download/enqueue
tasks.description: Accepts DownloadRequest; calls DownloadQueue.enqueue(req); returns {job_id: str, phase: 'queued', position: int} synchronously. ≤200ms; no yt-dlp work. 503 if yt-dlp/ffmpeg missing.

tasks.title: POST /api/download/album/enqueue
tasks.description: Same shape: accepts DownloadAlbumRequest, returns {job_id, phase: 'queued', position}.

tasks.title: GET /api/download/stream/{job_id} SSE
tasks.description:
StreamingResponse text/event-stream. Subscribe to DownloadQueue.event_stream(job_id). Emit `: keepalive

` every 15s as comment-line. Each event from worker → `data: <json>

`. On worker `done`, mark cache[job_id].consumed=True, close stream. If job_id unknown OR consumed: 410 Gone with {error_code:'already_consumed', path, status} for cached jobs / 404 unknown.
pass_criteria: After done, second GET returns 410 with cached path
Stream emits `: keepalive` every 15s in absence of events

tasks.title: POST /api/download/cancel/{job_id}
tasks.description: Sync + idempotent. Calls DownloadQueue.cancel(job_id) (sets cancel_event OR adds to cancel_pending). Returns 200 {cancelled:true} or 404 {cancelled:false, reason:'unknown_job'}.
pass_criteria: POST /api/download/cancel/{active_id} returns 200 + worker emits cancelled within 5s

tasks.title: GET /api/download/queue
tasks.description: Returns {active: {id,title,percent,phase,started_at,last_event_at}|null, queued: [{id,title,enqueued_at}], max_concurrency: int, downloads_per_minute_cap: int|null}.
pass_criteria: GET /api/download/queue with no jobs returns {active:null, queued:[], max_concurrency:1}
After enqueue, GET returns active populated with started_at

tasks.title: POST /api/download/reveal
tasks.description: Accepts RevealPathRequest (DL-033 handles validation). Branches by sys.platform: darwin → subprocess.run(['open','-R',path]); win32 → subprocess.run(['explorer.exe','/select,',path]); linux → subprocess.run(['xdg-open', os.path.dirname(path)]). Returns 204. If _reveal_supported() returns False → 501 reveal_unsupported_platform.

tasks.title: Extend GET /api/download/config
tasks.description: Add os_reveal_supported: bool (from _reveal_supported() probe) and max_concurrency: int (from DownloadQueue) to the existing response.
pass_criteria: GET /api/download/config response includes both new fields
DownloadConfigResponse model has the new fields

tasks.title: Add _reveal_supported() shutil.which probe
tasks.description: Per PRD §6.10: darwin → shutil.which('open'); win32 → shutil.which('explorer.exe'); linux → shutil.which('xdg-open'); else False. Module-level memoization (compute once).
pass_criteria: _reveal_supported() returns True on macOS dev box
monkeypatch shutil.which to None on Linux → returns False

tasks.title: Keep deprecated POST /api/download + /album back-compat
tasks.description: Maintain existing POST /api/download and POST /api/download/album as one-shot aliases: internally enqueue + open stream + emit. First SSE event is `{type:'queued', job_id, ...}` instead of starting with `progress`. Add @deprecated header in OpenAPI docstring; log INFO once per process.
pass_criteria: Existing E2E test for POST /api/download (single track) still passes
First SSE event from the legacy alias contains job_id

tasks.title: Add _Download IIFE skeleton
tasks.description: New IIFE near _consumeSSE def (~line 4550). Owns: state machine ({idle, loading, success, error}), AbortController per job, classified-error renderer, view-bind helpers. Exposed as window._Download for surface attachment. Stub bind methods; real implementations in DL-058+.

tasks.title: _Download.start() public API
tasks.description: POST /api/download/enqueue → get job_id → GET /api/download/stream/{job_id} → _consumeSSE → call onState({phase,percent,processed,total,currentTitle,path,classifiedError,raw}). Returns {id, cancel}. AbortController passed to both fetches.
pass_criteria: vitest: mock fetch, assert enqueue called THEN stream called THEN onState called with phase events
vitest: cancel() calls fetch /api/download/cancel/{id} AND aborts the stream

tasks.title: job.cancel() implementation
tasks.description: async () => { try { await fetch(`/api/download/cancel/${job_id}`, {method:'POST'}); } finally { ctrl.abort(); } }. Idempotent (multiple calls safe).
pass_criteria: vitest: calling cancel twice does not throw

tasks.title: Handle 410 already_consumed in stream
tasks.description: Per PRD §7.3: if stream.status === 410, read JSON body, render success/error/idle from cached payload WITHOUT toast. Goes before generic !stream.ok throw.
pass_criteria: vitest: mock GET /stream returning 410 with cached path → onState({phase:'done', status:'success', path, fromCache:true}); no toast call

tasks.title: Classified-error renderer with [Retry] and details
tasks.description:
Renders error_code + error_message + error_hint as primary text, [Retry] button calling _Download.start(originalArgs),
Show technical details
{error_raw}
. role=alert + aria-live=assertive on container.
pass_criteria: vitest: render error → query [data-action=retry]; click → start called again with same args

tasks.title: Disable Normalize toggle when format=original
tasks.description: On #dl-format change: if value === 'original' → set #dl-normalize.disabled=true, uncheck it, set aria-disabled=true, show tooltip 'Available only for WAV / MP3 320'. Else re-enable.
pass_criteria: vitest: change format to 'original' → normalize disabled + unchecked
vitest: change format back to 'mp3_320' → normalize re-enabled

tasks.title: Auto-retry on normalize_unsupported_for_original (capped at 1)
tasks.description: On enqueue 422 with error_code='normalize_unsupported_for_original': if !job._retriedNormalizeFlip → set format='mp3_320' (UI + localStorage), toast 'Normalization requires MP3 320 or WAV — switched to MP3 320', job._retriedNormalizeFlip=true, retry. Else surface error normally.
pass_criteria: vitest: mock 422 → retry called once with format=mp3_320; second 422 on retry → error rendered, no third call

tasks.title: _classifyDownloadTarget(q) URL classifier
tasks.description: Returns 'single_video' | 'playlist' | 'mixed_video_in_playlist' | 'search' | 'invalid'. Regex-based: ?v= without list → single_video; ?list= without v= → playlist; both → mixed; bare term → search; empty/multi-line/garbage → invalid.
pass_criteria: vitest: 5 fixtures cover each return value

tasks.title: _Download.bindManualPanel(rootEl)
tasks.description: Wires #dl-go-btn click + Enter submit → _Download.start({query: #dl-query.value, format: #dl-format.value, normalize: #dl-normalize.checked, embedMeta: #dl-embed-meta.checked, dest: _dlDestDir, allow_playlist: detected from classifier}). onState renders into #dl-status-region. Probes /api/download/config to show/hide #download-unavailable.
pass_criteria: vitest with JSDOM: render manual panel, type URL, click Download → enqueue fetch called
vitest: config response with available:false → #download-unavailable visible, controls hidden

tasks.title: _Download.bindCardButton(btnEl, query, opts)
tasks.description: Replaces existing .disc-dl-btn delegated handler at line 6963. Click → _Download.start({query, ...opts}). onState updates inline 90px progress bar + button label ('Downloading…' → '✓ saved' → 'Download again').
pass_criteria: vitest: click .disc-dl-btn → enqueue called; progress events update bar width

tasks.title: _Download.bindTrackInfoFlow(btnEl, query)
tasks.description: Wires #ti-download click → opens #yt-modal pre-populated with the track query. Modal then uses _Download.bindYoutubeModal for the actual fetch.

tasks.title: _Download.bindYoutubeModal(modalEl)
tasks.description: Replaces the existing _ytDownload hand-rolled SSE parser (~line 10807-10858). Pick button calls _Download.start({query: candidate.url}). Per-candidate inline progress; non-selected rows dim; Pick buttons disable during download. Rename 'Pick' → 'Download this version'.
pass_criteria: vitest: click Pick → enqueue called with candidate URL

tasks.title: _Download.bindConfirmModal(modalEl, payload)
tasks.description: Opens #disc-v2-dl-confirm with name/meta from payload. Cancel keeps default focus. Primary disabled 250ms after open. Don't-ask checkbox writes sessionStorage. On confirm → _Download.start(payload).
pass_criteria: vitest: open modal → primary disabled at t=0, enabled at t=250+
vitest: check don't-ask → next Shift+click skips modal (sessionStorage set)

tasks.title: Delete runDownload, downloadManual, _ytDownload
tasks.description: Remove the three old functions and their wirings. All call sites now go through _Download.

tasks.title: WAV-first-time inline explainer
tasks.description: On first WAV selection in a session, render a dismissible inline note under the format dropdown: 'WAV is uncompressed but the YouTube source is lossy — file is large (~5× MP3) and not actually lossless audio. Use this only if you need PCM for downstream tools.' Persisted in sessionStorage (autocue_dl_seen_wav_warning).
pass_criteria: vitest: change format to wav first time → note visible; dismiss → sessionStorage set; reload session → second wav selection shows note again

tasks.title: localStorage autocue_dl_format persistence + 192-coerce-toast
tasks.description: Read localStorage.autocue_dl_format on load; default 'mp3_320' if absent. On change, write. If stored value is legacy ('mp3', 'm4a', etc.), coerce per backend map AND show one-time toast 'Your saved format is now MP3 320 kbps. Change in the Format dropdown.'
pass_criteria: vitest: localStorage='mp3' on load → format = mp3_320, toast called once

tasks.title: seen_done_for_job: Set defense-in-depth
tasks.description: Module-level Set<job_id>. On every 'done' event from onState, check membership; if present, ignore. Add. Defense against duplicate done events that slip past server idempotency.
pass_criteria: vitest: emit two consecutive done events for same job_id → onState called once with done

tasks.title: #dl-queue-cancel-all + per-job cancel-queued
tasks.description: #dl-queue-cancel-all click → for each queued job_id in last GET /api/download/queue response, fire POST /api/download/cancel/{id}. Per-card 'Cancel queued' (when btn label shows 'Queued (N)') calls cancel for that job_id only.
pass_criteria: vitest: with 3 queued jobs, click cancel-all → 3 POST /cancel calls

tasks.title: visibilitychange pauses queue poll
tasks.description: document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', ...) toggles the 2s poll interval. document.hidden → clearInterval; visible → fire one immediate fetch + restart interval.
pass_criteria: vitest: dispatch visibilitychange with hidden=true → no further fetch /queue; hidden=false → one immediate fetch

tasks.title: Enter-to-submit on #dl-query
tasks.description: #dl-query.addEventListener('keydown', e => { if (e.key==='Enter' && !e.shiftKey) { e.preventDefault(); document.getElementById('dl-go-btn').click(); } }).
pass_criteria: vitest: type URL + dispatch Enter keydown → enqueue called

tasks.title: Esc precedence logic
tasks.description: Document keydown listener: (1) if topmost modal is open → close it (existing handlers); (2) else if activeElement is inside #download-section OR is <body> AND in-flight job exists on manual panel → show toast 'Press Esc again to cancel download'; second Esc within 1.5s → job.cancel(). Esc inside #dl-query alone preserves native blur.
pass_criteria: vitest: focus on #dl-query, no job → Esc does NOT call cancel
vitest: focus on body, job in flight → 1st Esc toast, 2nd Esc within 1.5s cancels
vitest: 2nd Esc after 1.5s does NOT cancel

tasks.title: Replace #download-section markup per PRD §8.2
tasks.description: Full HTML rewrite preserving #download-unavailable, #dl-dest-switch, adding #dl-format select, #dl-normalize / #dl-embed-meta toggles, #dl-queue-indicator, #dl-status-region (role=status aria-live=polite), <details> legal disclosure.

tasks.title: Add .dl-row/.dl-toggle/.dl-target CSS
tasks.description: Per PRD §8.5. .dl-row flex+gap. .dl-toggle inline-flex + min-height/min-width:24px + padding 2px 4px. #dl-format/#dl-go-btn/#dl-dest-switch min-height/min-width:24px. .dl-status-card padding+border+state-driven border-color (data-state=error|success).
pass_criteria: Playwright: getBoundingClientRect for every download interactive control returns width≥24 height≥24

tasks.title: Mobile @media (max-width:640px) Download layout
tasks.description: Stack input + format + button + toggles full-width; 44px min-height on each. Destination path RTL ellipsis. #download-section.collapsed becomes 48px strip. Touch targets 44x44.
pass_criteria: Playwright at 390x844: #dl-go-btn has full row width + ≥44px height
Playwright at 390: collapsed strip is visible at bottom; tap expands

tasks.title: @supports selector(:has(*)) guard + class-toggle fallback
tasks.description: Wrap the body:has(...) selectors in @supports. Inside @supports not (selector(:has(*))) { /* fallback: JS toggles body classes ds-strip-collapsed, db-visible, ab-visible on the matching state changes */ }. Add the JS toggles (one-liners on the relevant DOM mutations).
pass_criteria: Playwright in non-:has browser (or simulated) → fallback class-based selectors work

tasks.title: :root CSS vars + body:has selectors for bottom stack
tasks.description: Define --ds-strip-h, --db-h, --ab-h, --safe-bottom. body:has(#download-section.collapsed), body:has(#download-bar.visible), body:has(#action-bar[aria-hidden=false]) set bottom: calc(...) per PRD §6.9 ladder.
pass_criteria: Playwright mobile: #action-bar visible AND #download-bar.visible AND #download-section.collapsed all reachable; no overlap

tasks.title: Replace existing --ab-rest-y math with var-driven model
tasks.description: Delete the body:has(#download-bar.visible) #action-bar { --ab-rest-y: -66px; } block at ~line 906; replaced by DL-084's var system. Verify mobile stacking unchanged with no Download section visible.
pass_criteria: Existing E2E for action-bar layout still passes

tasks.title: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block
tasks.description: .dl-progress-fill { transition:none; } and any hover transforms disabled.

tasks.title: Restyle #disc-v2-dl-confirm
tasks.description: Per PRD §8.3. Body shows name + meta from payload. Buttons reversed: Cancel ghost (secondary-btn), Download album primary (green). Cancel keeps default focus. Primary disabled 250ms post-open. Don't-ask checkbox + 'Always skip in Settings →' tertiary link.
pass_criteria: Playwright: Shift+click card → modal shows 'Download album <name>?'; Cancel focused; primary disabled briefly

tasks.title: Settings 'Download' block + persistent skip-confirm checkbox
tasks.description:
Append to #settings-section:
Download
+ . Two-way mirror: checking writes localStorage AND sessionStorage; unchecking clears both.
pass_criteria: vitest: check persistent → both storages set; uncheck → both cleared

tasks.title: Focus trap + Escape + focus-return on #yt-modal
tasks.description: Reuse pattern from #disc-v2-dl-confirm (cyclic Tab among focusable elements within modal, Escape closes + cancels in-flight, focus returns to invoker btn).
pass_criteria: Playwright: open YT modal, Tab cycles within; Escape closes; focus returns to invoker

tasks.title: Replace _ytDownload SSE parser with _consumeSSE
tasks.description: Covered by DL-061 bind helper; this task is the cleanup: remove the ad-hoc parser at lines 10827-10853.

tasks.title: Rename 'Pick' → 'Download this version'
tasks.description: In #yt-candidates render template (~line 10800), change button text from 'Pick' to 'Download this version'.

tasks.title: Rename #download-btn → 'Export XML'
tasks.description: Button text + tooltip + any user-visible references. Line 3234 + downstream.

tasks.title: Rename #backup-btn → '💾 Save backup XML'
tasks.description: Line 2139. Also update 'Download backup first →' link (line 2164) to 'Save a backup first →'.

tasks.title: Step-4 wizard label + downstream copy
tasks.description: Step 4 of the wizard (line 2078): 'Download' → 'Export'. Line 2407 copy: 'Download the updated XML…' → 'Export the updated XML…'.

tasks.title: Shift+D shortcut + #kbd-overlay entry
tasks.description: Global keydown: e.shiftKey && e.key==='D' && !inInput → switch to Discover tab + scroll #download-section into view + focus #dl-query. Add row to #kbd-overlay: '⇧+D — Focus download'.
pass_criteria: Playwright: from Cues tab press Shift+D → URL hash becomes #discover, #dl-query.focus()

tasks.title: Reveal in Finder action
tasks.description: Success card [Reveal in Finder] button → fetch POST /api/download/reveal {path}. On 204 → no-op (Finder opens). On 403/501 → toast 'Couldn't open Finder'. Hidden when os_reveal_supported=false from /api/download/config.
pass_criteria: vitest: click Reveal → POST /api/download/reveal called with path

tasks.title: Copy path action
tasks.description: navigator.clipboard.writeText(path) → toast 'Path copied'. Fallback to old execCommand on browsers without clipboard API (none in supported baseline, but defensive).
pass_criteria: vitest: mock clipboard → click Copy → writeText called with path

tasks.title: Download another action
tasks.description: Per PRD §6.6: clear #dl-query.value, refocus input. Preserve format/normalize/embed_metadata (sticky prefs). Replace success card with idle state.
pass_criteria: vitest: render success → click Download another → query cleared, focus on input, format unchanged

tasks.title: Hide Reveal when os_reveal_supported=false
tasks.description: Read _downloadConfig.os_reveal_supported (set during config probe). When false, success-card renderer omits the Reveal button.
pass_criteria: vitest: config response with os_reveal_supported:false → success card has no Reveal button

tasks.title: #dl-queue-indicator polling /api/download/queue
tasks.description: Interval ID stored on module. Start when first job enqueued, stop when queue empty. Renders 'N active · M queued (max C concurrent)' from response. Polling gated by document.visibilityState (handled in DL-068).
pass_criteria: vitest: enqueue → poll starts; queue empty response → poll stops

tasks.title: #dl-queue-cancel-all wiring
tasks.description: Covered by DL-067 (separate task tracks cancel-all button vs per-job cancel-queued).

tasks.title: Add label-for on all download inputs
tasks.description: <label for=dl-query>, <label for=dl-format>, .dl-toggle wraps the checkbox AND text (implicit association). #yt-query needs an aria-label='YouTube search query' (no visible label in design).
pass_criteria: axe-core: 0 label-for violations on download surfaces

tasks.title: Replace #dl-progress div with <progress> element
tasks.description: + a visually-hidden Download progress. Replaces the .cue-tools-progress div wrapping.

tasks.title: aria-live=polite on #dl-status-region
tasks.description: Container that hosts loading/success/error UI gets aria-live=polite + aria-atomic=true. Progress percent updates throttled to ≥ 500ms to avoid spam.
pass_criteria: vitest: rapid percent updates → live region innerText changes ≤ 2× per second

tasks.title: role=alert + aria-live=assertive on error sub-region
tasks.description: The error renderer's outer container gets role=alert + aria-live=assertive so VoiceOver interrupts to read it. Focus also moves to the error container on render.
pass_criteria: vitest: render error → container has role=alert; document.activeElement is the container

tasks.title: aria-busy=true on container during loading
tasks.description: #download-controls.setAttribute('aria-busy', 'true') on phase=fetching/converting/normalizing/tagging; remove on done.
pass_criteria: vitest: state=loading → aria-busy='true'; state=idle → aria-busy absent

tasks.title: aria-disabled instead of disabled on Download btn during loading
tasks.description: Native `disabled` removes the button from a11y nav. Use aria-disabled='true' + a CSS rule [aria-disabled=true] { pointer-events:none; opacity:.5 } to disable interaction while keeping focusability.

tasks.title: Focus trap on #disc-v2-dl-confirm
tasks.description: Reuse trap pattern from existing modal code (the file already has one). Verify initial focus on Cancel; Escape closes + focus returns to invoker; Tab cycles within modal.
pass_criteria: Playwright: Shift+click card → Tab → focus on primary → Tab → focus on Cancel (cycle)

tasks.title: <fieldset><legend>Audio options</legend>… around format + toggles
tasks.description: Wrap #dl-format + #dl-normalize + #dl-embed-meta in a <fieldset> with visually-hidden <legend>Audio options</legend>. Groups the controls for SR users.

tasks.title: axe-core CI fixture + script
tasks.description: Add tests/e2e/test_axe_download_states.spec.ts running axe-core against /discover Download surfaces in 4 states (idle, loading, success, error). 0 violations required. Use @axe-core/playwright.

tasks.title: tests/test_classify_download_error.py — parametrized
tasks.description: @pytest.mark.parametrize over files in tests/fixtures/download_errors/. For each fixture: build an Exception (DownloadError(text) or matching OSError), call classify_download_error, assert ['code'] matches the fixture filename.
pass_criteria: pytest tests/test_classify_download_error.py passes for all 10 fixtures

tasks.title: Populate the 10 fixtures (real strings)
tasks.description: Already covered by DL-020. This task is the dedicated test-side responsibility line: ensure fixtures are non-empty + cite source URLs.
pass_criteria: for f in tests/fixtures/download_errors/*.txt; do [ -s $f ] && grep -q '^#' $f; done

tasks.title: tests/test_download_queue.py
tasks.description: Tests: concurrent enqueue with cap=1; cancel-before-pickup race; worker crash → emits done(worker_crash) + queue advances; watchdog kill after worker_pulse silence > 60s; legitimate long-job (mock pulse every 5s) survives watchdog.
pass_criteria: pytest tests/test_download_queue.py passes; 5 named tests; total runtime < 30s with sped-up timers

tasks.title: tests/test_download_format_coercion.py
tasks.description: Tests: POST enqueue {audio_format:'mp3'} → 200 + log WARNING (first time) + DEBUG (second); 'm4a' → coerced to 'original'; 'gibberish' → 422 unknown_format; 'audio_quality':'192' → 422 audio_quality_removed.

tasks.title: tests/test_download_normalize_lufs.py
tasks.description: Tests: synthesize a 5s sine wav at -23 LUFS using ffmpeg; run normalize_loudness_to_lufs; re-measure with ffmpeg loudnorm pass-1; assert ≤ -14 LUFS ± 1. Cancel test: set cancel_event after pass-1 starts, assert proc exits within 3s.

tasks.title: tests/test_download_reveal.py
tasks.description: Tests: POST /reveal {path: tmp_dir under default_download_dir} → 204; {path: '/etc/passwd'} → 422 forbidden_path; {path: non-existent} → 422/404; mock _reveal_supported=False → 501. Don't actually open Finder in test (mock subprocess.run).

tasks.title: tests/test_download_jargon.py
tasks.description: Greps user-visible strings in docs/index.html (heuristic: text inside elements that aren't <code>/<pre>/<!-- --> blocks) for 'yt-dlp', 'ffmpeg', 'exit code'. Whitelist: #download-unavailable banner (allowed to mention the install command). Test fails if jargon leaks elsewhere.

tasks.title: tests/test_download_sse_schema.py
tasks.description: Captures every SSE event emitted by /api/download/stream + /api/download/album/enqueue path. For each, DownloadProgressEvent.model_validate(json) must succeed. Catches drift between server emitter and schema.
pass_criteria: pytest tests/test_download_sse_schema.py passes with ≥ 5 distinct event shapes

tasks.title: tests/test_download_playlist.py
tasks.description: Mock expand_playlist to return [{query: 'http://a'}, {query: 'http://b'}]. POST /api/download/enqueue {query: '<playlist URL>', allow_playlist:True} → SSE includes events with processed in {1,2} and total=2.

tasks.title: tests/integration/test_download_classify_live.py
tasks.description: Gated by RUN_LIVE_YTDLP=1. For each canonical fixture URL listed in docs/reference/youtube-download.md#yt-dlp-upgrade, run yt-dlp.YoutubeDL().extract_info, catch the DownloadError, call classify_download_error, assert code matches expected. Also meta-test: assert codes are distinct across the suite.

tasks.title: tests/web/test_download_component.spec.js
tasks.description: Vitest: mock fetch; drive _Download.start through all 4 states (idle→loading→success); assert onState called with expected payloads at each transition.

tasks.title: tests/web/test_download_format_persistence.spec.js
tasks.description: Vitest: localStorage='wav' on load → #dl-format.value=='wav'. Change to 'mp3_320' → localStorage updated. Legacy value 'mp3' on load → coerced + toast called.

tasks.title: tests/web/test_download_410_already_consumed.spec.js
tasks.description: Vitest: mock GET /stream returning {status:410, json:()=>{path:'/x', status:'success'}}. _Download.start → onState called with {status:'success', path:'/x', fromCache:true}; no toast invoked.

tasks.title: tests/web/test_consume_sse_keepalive.spec.js
tasks.description: Vitest: feed _consumeSSE a Response stream containing ': keepalive\n\n' between two data: events. Assert onEvent called exactly twice (heartbeats ignored).

tasks.title: tests/e2e/test_download_e2e.spec.ts — single track happy path
tasks.description: Playwright: navigate to /discover, type valid URL, click Download, await success state, assert path visible. Mock the backend with a short fake audio URL or use a real Creative Commons URL.

tasks.title: tests/e2e/test_download_cancel.spec.ts
tasks.description: Playwright: start download, wait for progress > 5%, click Cancel, assert state returns to idle within 5s. Verify backend log shows DownloadCancelled. Verify partial file removed.

tasks.title: tests/e2e/test_download_per_card.spec.ts
tasks.description: Playwright: navigate to /discover, wait for ≥ 1 card with .disc-dl-btn, click it, assert inline progress bar appears + button label flips to 'Downloading…'. (May need to seed discover.db with a test card.)

tasks.title: tests/e2e/test_download_mobile.spec.ts
tasks.description: Playwright at 390x844: navigate to /discover, assert collapsed Download strip is visible without scroll. Tap strip → full panel appears. Assert all touch targets ≥ 44x44.

tasks.title: tests/e2e/test_download_confirm_modal.spec.ts
tasks.description: Playwright: Shift+click a Discover card. Assert modal opens with name + meta. Cancel has default focus. Primary disabled at t=0, enabled by t=300. Check dontask → next Shift+click skips modal. Settings persistent checkbox → next session also skips.

tasks.title: tests/e2e/test_axe_download_states.spec.ts
tasks.description: Covered by DL-118; this row tracks the test-suite side. Run axe-core against /discover in 4 simulated states; assert 0 violations each.

tasks.title: tests/e2e/test_download_queue_concurrency.spec.ts
tasks.description: Playwright: enqueue 20 jobs (e.g. via 20 .disc-dl-btn clicks in rapid succession). Assert /api/download/queue shows 1 active + 19 queued. Cancel-all → all jobs return cancelled within 5s.

tasks.title: Update CLAUDE.md must-know constraints
tasks.description: Add a Download row to 'Must-know constraints' covering: AUTOCUE_DOWNLOAD_CONCURRENCY env, job-queue + cancel-endpoint architecture, ffmpeg subprocess.Popen.terminate(), output_artifacts mtime gate, 410 already_consumed semantics, format coercion mapping. Also add to 'Depth — read on demand' a row pointing to docs/reference/youtube-download.md.

tasks.title: Update docs/FEATURES.md Download section
tasks.description: Rewrite the Download section to describe the new flow: format selector, loudness normalize, ID3/RIFF tagging, playlist batching, classified errors, Reveal in Finder. Match copy with PRD.

tasks.title: Flesh out docs/reference/youtube-download.md
tasks.description: Beyond the scaffold from DL-003: write the format-migration table, the 4-step yt-dlp upgrade checklist, browser support details, canonical fixture URLs with capture-date, troubleshooting for stuck jobs / disk-full / age-gated.

tasks.title: Chrome DevTools end-to-end verification
tasks.description: Boot autocue serve. Drive Chrome DevTools through every Download state across all 5 surfaces (manual panel idle/loading/success/error/unavailable, per-card, Shift+click confirm, track-info, YT modal) on desktop (1280x900) AND mobile (390x844). Save screenshots to allowed temp dir. SendUserFile each. Verify 0 console errors. Final acceptance.
pass_criteria: Screenshots cover all 5 surfaces × 4-5 states × 2 viewports
Every §12 acceptance gate verified manually or with cited test
